title: StrategicReviewOutput
description: Model-mediated output of the strategic-staleness reviewer. Every per-entry field here is a model-owned judgment (question, action, confidence, classification, rationale). Code validates the contract and renders it; code never generates these judgments. The auto-revise gate may only act on entries the model classified as objective_drift with recommended_action revise, and only ever proposes (execution requires operator approval routed through governance). This build phase never turns the gate on.

decision.description: The reviewer's overall decision for this packet.

entries.description: One judgment per surfaced strategic claim, in the HYBRID shape: a natural-language question AND a recommended action, plus evidence, confidence, and an objective_drift/uncertain classification.

revise_proposals.description: Generated only when the auto-revise gate is ON and the model classified a claim as objective_drift with recommended_action revise. Always pending approval; never auto-executed.

auto_revise_enabled.description: Reflects the gate state at run time. Must be false for every packet produced this build phase.

entity_id.description: First-class join key for entity-current-state claims. Carried verbatim from the source card by a code-owned join (never model-produced) so a consumer joins entry.entity_id == card.entity_id without parsing scope_key. Absent on non-entity entries.

nl_question.description: Natural-language question for Braydon about whether this strategic claim still holds.

recommended_action.description: validate = confirm it still holds; revise = it has drifted, update it; retire = it is superseded/dead, remove it.

classification.description: Model-owned: is this a verifiable drift (a referenced fact has changed, a stated priority has been superseded in a newer document), or does it need human judgment?
